review: I’ve used Spotify for 2 years now and I’ve never had premium, and I’ve noticed just how painful it is to use Spotify without premium. First of all, you can’t listen to 1 song without having to listen 3 or 4 ads, all 30 second ads, meaning you listen to ads for like 5 minutes btw. And, Once you finish a song, you immediately listen to like 5 ads, it’s actually ridiculous. And I get the whole “we have to pay artists” thing, but it’s not just the ads that is stupid. For some reason, you can’t rewind, skip more then 6 songs, or really do anything without Spotify premium,  it’s actually horrific, not to mention this whole “newish” thing where if you search and play one too many songs when searching, it prevents you from playing a song you want dand plays a random song that you didn’t pick instead and suggests you get premium. Genuinely how desperate can an app be for a membership… my advice is just use YouTube, they have WAYYYY less ads and it’s just better, Spotify has gotten so bad that’s it not even worth it anymore. The only reason it gets 2 stars is because listening to podcasts on Spotify is actually really nice, and because you get Spotify wrapped. Otherwise, it’s kind of useless, just use YouTube instead!!

review: Spotify is easy to use and nice, as long as you have Premium. Got the free trial, and the overall experience was flawless, though I wish they had an automated flawless AutoMix feature like Apple Music. On the other hand, free Spotify is a nightmare and makes some feel miserable. Ads are fine, sometimes the same ad repeats twice in a row which is a little annoying, but getting ads every less than the 15 minutes advertised is more annoying. Another thing is the fixed shuffle mode. Artists carefully craft album tracklists for everyone to enjoy, but then Spotify ruins that and puts unrelated tracks on top of that. Additionally, Spotify also forces in unrelated songs that you as an user haven’t listened to into a public playlist you may be listening to, once I got 12 tracks (equal to 40mins or more) before Spotify queued up the songs left from the playlist. Skips are useful for those cases, but those are still very limited and capped at 6 per hour. As a user, you have to find workarounds, like making a playlist with an album you may want to listen to instead of listening to it directly to avoid the unrelated songs from queueing up, but even that does not prevent anything if the playlist is short. The UI could improve too, maybe used Liquid Glass or just modernize it and make it look less full. Wish there was a paid tier below Premium that’s not the current student discount.

review:
So my Spotify app updated today on my iPhone, and now the “like” button is basically hidden unless I fully open the app, tap the little bar at the bottom showing what’s currently playing, and then open the full-screen player just to save a song. Seriously, why make something so simple so complicated?

For a music app, quick access to the like/add button should be basic functionality. I shouldn’t have to jump through multiple steps just to save a song I like. This update honestly made the app more frustrating to use, not better. 
& If saving a song now requires a whole side quest, I might as well cancel my subscription.

Amazing innovation, Spotify. Really groundbreaking stuff. NOT.

review: I love Spotify as much as the next guy and what I’ve been really excited to get for Spotify, is lossless audio. However, if you download way too many songs in lossless quality, it essentially eats your entire phone’s storage. So like any rational person, I downgraded the quality of my downloads to very high, but thought since I’m 99% of the time listening to music through WiFi or cellular data, that I could turn that to Lossless and would get that quality. Unfortunately, Spotify doesn’t work that way. So any song that I like enough to download to my phone, even if I’m connected to the internet and should have Lossless audio for it, is instead placed in Very High quality which is kind of frustrating. Just because I would like to listen to my favorite songs in Lossless, but also would like to have access to them in a slightly lesser quality in case I’m not connected to the internet without it eating my phones entire storage.

review:
Spotify has genuinely become one of the most important parts of my everyday life. Music isn’t just background noise to me — it’s therapy, healing, motivation, comfort, memories, and sometimes the only thing that understands emotions words can’t explain. Through every heartbreak, celebration, long drive, workout, work shift, sleepless night, and life-changing moment, Spotify has been there with the perfect song at the perfect time.

What I love most about Spotify is how personal it feels. The playlists, recommendations, daylists, and discover features somehow always know exactly what my soul needs to hear. One moment I’m dancing and feeling unstoppable, the next I’m crying to lyrics that feel like they were written directly from my heart. Music has the power to save people, and Spotify gives millions of people access to that feeling every single day.

The app itself is smooth, organized, and easy to use, but beyond the technology, it creates connection. Spotify helped me discover artists from all over the world, songs tied to memories I’ll never forget, and music that got me through some of the hardest periods of my life. There are songs I physically cannot listen to without remembering certain people, places, or moments — and that’s beautiful. That’s powerful.

I truly believe music is one of the keys to life. It brings people together regardless of language, culture, pain, or background. Spotify made music accessible in a way that transformed daily living for so many of us. Whether I’m driving by the ocean at night, getting ready for work at 4 AM, healing from loss, or celebrating life with friends, Spotify is always part of the experience.

Thank you for creating something that does more than stream songs. You created a platform that holds emotions, memories, healing, energy, love, and human connection. I honestly cannot imagine life without music, and I cannot imagine music without Spotify.
